item.title: Mexican governor accused by U.S. of drug trafficking temporarily steps down
item.description: The U.S. accuses Sinaloa Gov. Ruben Rocha Moya of working with cartels to distribute "massive quantities" of narcotics to the United States.

item.title: Finance tips for when you're caring for aging family members
item.description: "CBS Saturday Morning" shares tips on managing your finances when you're caring for aging family members.

item.title: Trump "not satisfied" with new peace deal offered by Iran
item.description: President Trump said on Friday he did not like Iran's new proposal to end the war as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Ford CEO discusses auto industry affordability amid rising gas prices and economic uncertainty
item.description: The average cost of a new car is about 30% more expensive than 2019, data shows. Ford CEO Jim Farley spoke with "CBS Saturday Morning" about how the car company is tackling affordability amid economic uncertainty and rising gas prices.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shuts down after failing to secure government bailout
item.description: Spirit Airlines announced early Saturday morning that it would immediately cease operations after failing to secure a last-minute government bailout.

item.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after failed effort at government rescue deal
item.description: The budget carrier Spirit Airlines is ceasing operations after failing to land a $500 million bailout from the Trump administration.

item.title: New video shows correspondents' dinner suspect breaching event security
item.description: New video shows the alleged White House Correspondents' Dinner shooter breaching event security after roaming the hotel hallways prior to the dinner.

item.title: Trump rejects Iran's latest peace proposal as gas prices skyrocket
item.description: President Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal for a peace deal on Friday, saying he was "not satisfied." Meanwhile, gas prices have hit a four-year high, according to AAA.

item.title: What will a Spirit Airlines shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights as the budget airline ceases operations.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a field of 19 horses today in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: The Killing of Theresa Fusco | Post Mortem
item.description: "48 Hours" correspondents Anne-Marie Green and Erin Moriarty discuss the cases of Theresa Fusco, Kelly Morrissey, and Jackie Martarella, who went missing more than 40 years ago in Long Island, New York.

item.title: Starmer urges action at some Gaza protests after antisemitic attacks
item.description: Keir Starmer said he would always defend the right to protest, but that there may be instances where some marches​ should be banned.

item.title: Football practice was leaving a teen fatigued. A rare cancer was to blame.
item.description: Cameron Rider's fatigue, body aches and fever were diagnosed as pneumonia, but he couldn't seem to get better.

item.title: Operation Neptune Spear: Killing bin Laden | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2012, Scott Pelley spoke with a retired Navy SEAL, and former member of SEAL Team 6, who recounted the May 2011 raid on Osama bin Laden's compound in Abbottabad, Pakistan.

item.title: FDA expands access to drug helping pancreatic cancer patients
item.description: Former Sen. Ben Sasse, 54, called daraxonrasib "a miracle drug" that was allowing him to live longer and with less pain.

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down as soon as Saturday
item.description: Negotiations over a $500 million dollar government aid package for Spirit stalled after bondholders balked at the terms.

item.title: Trump tightens U.S. sanctions on Cuba, signaling a warning to the island, expert says
item.description: The new measures increase pressure on foreign financial institutions by threatening their access to U.S. markets if they continue to work with Cuban government entities.

item.title: May Day protest footage from around the U.S.
item.description: Americans took to the streets on Friday for May Day, also referred to as International Workers' Day. The day commemorates the 1886 Haymarket Affair, when a Chicago protest for an eight-hour workday turned into a violent clash between demonstrators and police. Some cities saw "Workers Over Billionaires" rallies and marches this year.

item.title: Bard College president to retire after revelations of his ties to Epstein
item.description: The longtime president of Bard College announced his retirement, months after it was revealed that he had a much deeper relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein than was previously known.

item.title: What states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states, as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.

item.title: Who are the favorites, longshots heading into Kentucky Derby?
item.description: Saturday marks the 152nd running of the Kentucky Derby at Churchill Downs. 20 horses are set to run for the roses in the first leg of the triple crown. Hannah Vanbiber, a senior editor at The Athletic, joins "The Daily Report" to preview what's dubbed the most exciting two minutes in sports.

item.title: Tennessee, Alabama take steps to redraw House maps in wake of Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The Republican governors of Tennessee and Alabama called state lawmakers into special sessions on Friday, initial steps in what could be a scramble to redraw congressional maps after the Supreme Court narrowed the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: ICE reports 18th detainee death in 2026, putting agency on track for new record
item.description: ICE reported the 18th death of an individual in its custody so far this year, putting the agency on track to record a new all-time high in detainee deaths.

item.title: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated"
item.description: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.

item.title: Appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pill mifepristone in U.S.
item.description: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.

item.title: Pickleball Inc. $225 million deal creates new home for sport
item.description: The deal merged Major League Pickleball and the Carvana PPA Tour, two of the nascent sport's most active entities, under one company, Pickleball Inc.

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: 5/1: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Spirit Airlines prepares to end operations after failing to secure government rescue deal; Trump tells Congress hostilities in Iran have "terminated."

item.title: 5-year-old boy gets special gift; Artemis astronauts reveal if you can burp in space
item.description: The Artemis II crew surprised a 5-year-old boy space enthusiast and answered "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King's true or false questions about burping in space and whether you become taller in space. The crew appeared for a CBS News town hall "Artemis II: A Celebration of Heroes."

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.
